question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: hasBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user control a block node that returns firstAvailableBlock=-1 in getBlockRange(), causing hasBlock() to return false (EMPTY_BLOCK_RANGE check), so that the node is never selected and if all nodes return this, getNode() throws BlockStreamException, permanently halting staking reward block processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onBlockStream()] Can an unprivileged external user operate a block node that serves a block where the TSS signature is valid but the block content has been crafted to include a NodeStakeUpdateTransaction with manipulated staking reward rates, bypassing verifyTssSignature() if the attacker has compromised enough TSS key shares, causing EntityStakeCalculatorImpl to compute incorrect pendingReward values?
